content:
# Luke Chapter 1 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), Folios 42r-43v (PDF pages 83-85) | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/LUK_001_page_001.jpg, LUK_001_page_002.jpg, LUK_001_page_003.jpg

---

**1** Many have undertaken to compile a narrative of the events that have been fulfilled among us, **2** just as those who were eyewitnesses from the beginning and who became servants of the word handed them down to us. **3** Therefore, since I too have carefully traced everything from the very start, it seemed right to write an orderly account for you, most honored Theophilus, **4** so that you may know the certainty of the things you have been taught.

**5** In the days of Herod, king of Judea, there was a priest named Zechariah, belonging to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife was from the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth. **6** They were both righteous in God's sight, living blamelessly by all the Lord's commands and requirements. **7** But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and both of them were well along in years.

**8** Now while Zechariah was serving as priest before God during his division's turn, **9** he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter the sanctuary of the Lord and burn incense. **10** The whole assembly of the people was praying outside at the hour of incense.

**11** Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right side of the altar of incense. **12** When Zechariah saw him, he was shaken, and fear overwhelmed him. **13** But the angel said to him, "Do not be afraid, Zechariah, because your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you are to name him John. **14** He will be your joy and delight, and many will rejoice at his birth, **15** for he will be great in the Lord's sight. He must never drink wine or strong drink. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even from his mother's womb, **16** and he will turn many of the children of Israel back to the Lord their God. **17** He will go before the Lord in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of fathers toward their children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous — to prepare a people made ready for the Lord."

**18** Zechariah said to the angel, "How can I be sure of this? I am an old man, and my wife is well along in years." **19** The angel answered him, "I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news. **20** And now — listen — you will be silent and unable to speak until the day these things happen, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled at their appointed time."

**21** Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah and wondering why he was taking so long in the sanctuary. **22** When he came out, he could not speak to them, and they realized he had seen a vision in the sanctuary. He kept making signs to them but remained unable to speak.

**23** When the days of his priestly service were completed, he went home. **24** After those days his wife Elizabeth conceived, and for five months she kept herself in seclusion, saying, **25** "This is what the Lord has done for me in the days when he looked on me with favor, to take away my disgrace among people."

**26** In the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent by God to a town in Galilee called Nazareth, **27** to a virgin pledged in marriage to a man named Joseph, of the house of David. The virgin's name was Mary. **28** The angel came to her and said, "Greetings, you who are highly favored! The Lord is with you." **29** She was deeply troubled by his words and kept wondering what kind of greeting this could be. **30** The angel said to her, "Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God. **31** Listen: you will conceive in your womb and give birth to a son, and you are to name him Jesus. **32** He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father David, **33** and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever. His kingdom will never end."

**34** Mary said to the angel, "How will this be, since I have not been intimate with a man?" **35** The angel answered her, "The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. For this reason the holy one to be born will be called the Son of God. **36** And listen — your relative Elizabeth has also conceived a son in her old age. She who was called barren is now in her sixth month. **37** For nothing is impossible with God."

**38** Mary said, "Here I am, the Lord's servant. Let it happen to me as you have said." Then the angel left her.

**39** In those days Mary got up and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judah, **40** where she entered Zechariah's home and greeted Elizabeth. **41** When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit. **42** She cried out in a loud voice, "Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb! **43** And why am I so honored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me? **44** For the moment the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy. **45** Blessed is she who believed that what the Lord has spoken to her will be fulfilled!"

**46** And Mary said:

"My soul magnifies the Lord,
**47** and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,
**48** for he has looked with favor on the humble state of his servant.
From now on all generations will call me blessed,
**49** for the Mighty One has done great things for me —
holy is his name.
**50** His mercy extends to those who fear him,
from generation to generation.
**51** He has performed mighty deeds with his arm;
he has scattered those who are proud in the thoughts of their hearts.
**52** He has brought down rulers from their thrones
and lifted up the humble.
**53** He has filled the hungry with good things
and sent the rich away empty.
**54** He has helped his servant Israel,
remembering his mercy,
**55** just as he spoke to our fathers —
to Abraham and his offspring forever."

**56** Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months and then returned to her home.

**57** Now the time came for Elizabeth to give birth, and she bore a son. **58** Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy, and they celebrated with her.

**59** On the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they were going to name him Zechariah after his father. **60** But his mother spoke up and said, "No! He is to be called John." **61** They said to her, "There is no one among your relatives who has that name." **62** Then they made signs to his father, asking what he wanted to name the child. **63** He asked for a writing tablet and wrote, "His name is John." And everyone was astonished. **64** Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue set free, and he began to speak, praising God. **65** Fear came over all their neighbors, and throughout the entire hill country of Judea people were talking about all these things. **66** Everyone who heard about it took it to heart, saying, "What then will this child become?" For the Lord's hand was with him.

**67** His father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied:

**68** "Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel,
for he has visited and redeemed his people.
**69** He has raised up a horn of salvation for us
in the house of his servant David,
**70** just as he spoke through the mouth of his holy prophets long ago —
**71** salvation from our enemies
and from the hand of all who hate us,
**72** to show mercy to our fathers
and to remember his holy covenant,
**73** the oath he swore to our father Abraham:
**74** to rescue us from the hand of our enemies
so that we could serve him without fear,
**75** in holiness and righteousness before him
all our days.

**76** And you, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High,
for you will go before the Lord to prepare his ways,
**77** to give his people knowledge of salvation
through the forgiveness of their sins,
**78** because of the tender mercy of our God,
by which the dawn from on high will visit us,
**79** to shine on those sitting in darkness
and in the shadow of death,
to guide our feet into the way of peace."

**80** The child grew and became strong in spirit, and he lived in the wilderness until the day he appeared publicly to Israel.

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

1. **Verse 1 — "fulfilled" (pepleroph oremenon):** Sinaiticus reads ΠΕΠΛΗΡΟΦΟΡΗΜΕΝΩΝ. I rendered this as "fulfilled" rather than the more wooden "accomplished" or "been brought to completion." The word carries the sense of events reaching their destined fullness — things coming to pass as they were meant to. "Fulfilled" captures both the historical reality and the theological weight.

2. **Verse 3 — "most honored Theophilus" (kratiste Theophile):** The superlative ΚΡΑΤΙΣΤΕ is a formal address of social rank. "Most excellent" is traditional but sounds stilted in modern English. "Most honored" preserves the formality and social distance Luke intends without sounding archaic.

3. **Verse 17 — "to turn the hearts of fathers toward their children":** This echoes Malachi 4:6. I kept the Malachi phrasing recognizable rather than modernizing it further, since Luke clearly intends the allusion to resonate.

4. **Verse 20 — "And now — listen —":** The Greek ΚΑΙ ΙΔΟΥ (kai idou, "and behold") functions as an attention-grabber. Rather than the archaic "behold," I used the dash-interrupted "listen" to create the same dramatic pause and urgency the angel intends. Gabriel is about to pronounce judgment on Zechariah's unbelief.

5. **Verse 34 — "since I have not been intimate with a man":** The Greek ΟΥ ΓΙΝΩΣΚΩ ΑΝΔΡΑ uses the biblical euphemism "know a man." I rendered this with the modern equivalent euphemism "been intimate with" rather than the literal "do not know a man," which would be opaque to modern readers.

6. **Verse 48 — "humble state" (tapeinosin):** ΤΑΠΕΙΝΩΣΙΝ can mean "lowliness," "humiliation," or "humble condition." Mary is not claiming to be humiliated but acknowledging her low social standing. "Humble state" captures this social reality without implying shame.

7. **Verse 69 — "horn of salvation":** I retained this Hebraic metaphor rather than replacing it, because it is so deeply embedded in biblical tradition (echoing Hannah's song and the Psalms) that flattening it would lose more than it gains. The image of the horn as a symbol of strength and rescue is accessible enough in context.

8. **Verse 78 — "the dawn from on high" (anatolē ex hypsous):** Sinaiticus reads ΑΝΑΤΟΛΗ ΕΞ ΥΨΟΥΣ. This messianic image — a sunrise breaking from heaven — is both cosmic and intimate. "Dawn from on high" preserves the vertical dimension (it comes from above) and the imagery of light breaking into darkness, which verse 79 then expands.

9. **The Magnificat (vv. 46-55) and Benedictus (vv. 68-79):** These are hymnic, poetic passages. I formatted them with line breaks and preserved their parallelism and rhythm. The goal was to make them feel like songs — because they are songs — while keeping the English natural and speakable.

10. **Sinaiticus-specific readings:** I followed the Sinaiticus text where it diverges from later manuscripts. Notably, Sinaiticus does not include "blessed are you among women" in the angel's greeting at verse 28 (it appears only in Elizabeth's exclamation at verse 42), which I have reflected here. The manuscript's scriptio continua (continuous writing without word breaks) was read directly from the page images.

— PNEUMA
